description: A dish of very thinly sliced raw beef, usually served chilled and dressed lightly with olive oil, lemon juice, or another acidic dressing. It is commonly finished with Parmesan, arugula, capers, or pepper.
core_ingredients: Very fresh beef tenderloin or sirloin
preparation_steps: Use a high-quality, very fresh cut of beef and chill it until firm.
Slice it paper-thin or pound it gently between sheets to thin it further.
Arrange the slices on a cold plate.
Dress lightly just before serving and add garnishes such as Parmesan and arugula.

nutrition.fat: Low to moderate, depending on dressing

health_advice.benefits: High in protein and usually low in carbohydrates.
health_advice.risks: Because it contains raw beef, food-safety risk is the main concern, especially for pregnant people, young children, older adults, and immunocompromised people.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Use trusted, very fresh meat kept cold and prepare it hygienically; avoid it entirely if raw meat is medically inadvisable.

dietary_notes.note: The basic dish is meat-based and often gluten-free, but sauces or Worcestershire-style dressings may add allergens.

allergens: Dairy if Parmesan or creamy sauce is added
Egg if mayonnaise-based dressing is used
